name: labor_policy_procurement_shortcut_millhaven_jus_mer
premise: A vendor outage and an internal change window collided; rollback paths are not symmetric and both teams blame the other. In Labor Policy, the situation tagged internally around labor policy procurement shortcut millhaven jus mer has converged with local pressure in the Millhaven corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. Finance notes that the fastest mitigation spends down a restricted reserve that another program legally depends on next quarter. Key facts are still arriving from the field; the team is working off stale dashboards and verbal handoffs. A public briefing is scheduled; if no coherent position exists, staff will improvise talking points from conflicting drafts. The trade is not abstract: it is justice versus mercy under incomplete information, and whichever value you deprioritize will have defenders who are technically correct about the risk they fear.

agents.name: Camila Patel
agents.identity: I am Camila Patel, the senior manager who must reconcile legal, ops, and customer-facing reality in one decision in Labor Policy. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat mercy as disposable collateral every time justice spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Soren Morales
agents.identity: I am Soren Morales, the risk and equity liaison pushing back on silent harms in Labor Policy. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.
agents.goal: I want explicit guardrails so justice cannot become a blank check that quietly vaporizes mercy in the paperwork afterward.

choices: I will sequence a conservative rollout: smallest blast radius first, documented exceptions only, and real appeals paths—even if justice advocates call that foot-dragging.
I will push a fast, organization-wide lever because partial fixes may leave the worst harm untouched—even if that compresses protections for mercy.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Silence will be interpreted as a decision by everyone outside this room. Camila Patel must choose under justice versus mercy. What does Camila Patel do?
